channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams says NYC's Roosevelt Hotel migrant shelter will soon close
item.description: The massive Roosevelt Hotel migrant shelter in Manhattan will shut down in the next few months, New York City Mayor Eric Adams announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Conservatives win German election while far-right party surges to second place
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany's mainstream conservatives led by Friedrich Merz won Germany’s national election, ensuring that Ukraine has an even stronger supporter in the European Union's largest country and creating hopes for renewal in an economic powerhouse that has been badly battered in recent years.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek facing a deadline from Elon Musk to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces Dan Bongino as Deputy FBI Director
item.description: Bongino is a former U.S. Secret Service agent that became known for his defense of Trump while previously appearing as a Fox News contributor.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KREX 5

item.title: Pope awake, resting on 10th day of hospitalization
item.description: Doctors have said Francis’ condition is touch-and-go, given his age, fragility and pre-existing lung disease.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hospital patient breaks 'every bone' in nurse's face during attack: Police
item.description: A Florida man is facing attempted murder charges after allegedly attacking a nurse at a hospital where he was being cared for, essentially breaking every bone in her face.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-Secret Service agent and conservative media personality Dan Bongino picked as FBI deputy director
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dan Bongino, a former U.S. Secret Service agent who has penned best-selling books, ran unsuccessfully for office and gained fame as a conservative pundit with TV shows and a popular podcast, has been chosen to serve as FBI deputy director.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Witness saw Brian Laundrie acting 'like a lunatic' 2 weeks before Gabby Petito murder, FBI files reveal
item.description: Gabby Petito's killer boyfriend Brian Laundrie was seen acting "like a lunatic" two weeks before he killed her in Wyoming, according to notes from the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Coast Guard, New York first responders searching for missing person after boat capsized with six passengers
item.description: A search is underway for a missing person in the water near the coast of New York after a boat capsized with six people on it, leaving three dead and two hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline to list accomplishments looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Eric Tucker And Amanda Seitz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The UN will vote to demand Russia pull troops out of Ukraine. But the US wants a softer approach
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — On the third anniversary of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, the U.N. General Assembly is expected to vote Monday on dueling resolutions — Ukraine's European-backed proposal demanding an immediate withdrawal of Russian forces from the country and a U.S.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Greenpeace says a pipeline company's lawsuit threatens the organization's future
item.description: A Texas pipeline company has sued Greenpeace accusing the organization of defamation, disruptions and attacks during protests against the Dakota Access Pipeline.
item.dc:creator: Jack Dura, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jury selection to begin in murder trial of suspect in suburban Chicago July 4 parade
item.description: The man accused of opening fire on a suburban Chicago Independence Day parade and killing seven people is about to stand trial nearly three years after the attack.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mystics trade 2-time All-Star Ariel Atkins to the Sky for No. 3 pick in WNBA draft
item.description: The Washington Mystics accelerated their rebuild, trading two-time All-Star Ariel Atkins to the Chicago Sky for the No. 3 overall pick in this year's draft on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Key federal agencies refuse to comply with Musk's latest demand in his cost-cutting crusade
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Key U.S. agencies, including the FBI, State Department and the Pentagon, have instructed their employees not to comply with cost-cutting chief Elon Musk's latest demand that federal workers explain what they accomplished last week — or risk losing their job.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, ERIC TUCKER and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio bathroom law targeting transgender students has brought internal strife to some campuses
item.description: For some famously progressive colleges in Ohio, a new state law designed to keep transgender women from using women’s restrooms at schools is bringing a moment of soul-searching for students, alumni and administrators.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Woman accused of looting from home in Palisades Fire area while wearing 'Palisades Strong' shirt
item.description: California Highway Patrol officers arrested a woman accused of looting from a home in the Palisades Fire area while wearing a "Palisades Strong" shirt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American Airlines flight from New York to New Delhi lands safely in Rome after security concern
item.description: ROME (AP) — An American Airlines flight from New York to New Delhi landed safely in Rome on Sunday afternoon after it was diverted due to a security concern, which later proved to be “non-credible,” the airline said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Democratic governors balance whether to fight or pacify after Trump threatens one of their own
item.description: President Donald Trump’s real-time confrontation with Maine’s governor over transgender athletes captured the conundrum many Democratic governors are facing in the Republican’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis remains in critical condition with early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Zelenskyy offers to resign in exchange for peace or Ukraine’s NATO membership
item.description: “If it’s about peace in Ukraine, and you really want me to leave my position – I am ready to do that," Zelenskyy told reporters at a press conference in Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Luxury portable toilets sought after ahead of NFL Draft
item.description: The luxury restroom trailers and containers feature flushing toilets, sinks, air conditioning, and even stereo systems.
item.dc:creator: Timothy Littau, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope Francis remains in critical condition with early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lynne Marie Stewart of 'Pee-wee's Playhouse,' 'It's Always Sunny' dies: reports
item.description: The actress has recently finished filming for another season of "It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia" and her forthcoming pickleball-related film, "Dink.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Beware of text messages about unpaid tolls, FBI and state authorities warn
item.description: Drivers should be on the lookout for scam text messages warning of an unpaid road toll bill, according to recent warnings from authorities.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Connecticut cannibal killer given conditional release after 2011 murder in which he ate victim's brain, eye
item.description: An institutionalized man who confessed to the 2011 killing of a homeless man in Connecticut in which he also ate the victim's body parts was granted a conditional release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California governor asks Congress for nearly $40 billion for Los Angeles wildfire relief
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has asked Congress to approve nearly $40 billion in aid to help the Los Angeles area recover from January's devastating wildfires, which he said could become the costliest natural disaster in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: USPS honors beloved actress Betty White with commemorative stamp
item.description: The stamp features a digitally created portrait of White against a violet background with "bubbly spots that befit her sparkling personality.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Border Patrol agent whose death is tied to cultlike Zizians is buried with military honors
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent whose killing in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border has been tied to a cultlike group was buried with full military honors Saturday at a national cemetery in Minnesota. David Maland, 44, died Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Shooting at Air Force base gate leaves airman dead after off-base pursuit
item.description: A shooting near Kirtland Air Force Base in Albuquerque left one airman dead and another hospitalized early Saturday morning, the Air Force said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Shooting after pursuit at Air Force Base in New Mexico kills an airman and wounds another
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A shooting at a U.S. Air Force base in New Mexico early Saturday left one airman dead and another wounded, military officials said, adding that it was not an act of terrorism or an attack by an outsider.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pennsylvania Gov. Shapiro meets with family of fallen officer, victims of hospital shooting
item.description: "I am inspired by the amazing doctors, nurses, and staff at UPMC and WellSpan Health who did tremendous work to save lives in the face of tragedy," said Shapiro. "It was my honor to meet with them and to thank them for what they do in service to their neighbors.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, WGN 9

item.title: Memorial grows for Pennsylvania officer killed in hospital shooting
item.description: A memorial is growing outside the police department in West York, a borough outside of York, Pennsylvania, after officer Andrew Duarte was killed in the line of duty on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KLAS, CBS News
